summary:
Claim: Guiding cusps engage in embrasures.  
Evidence: - Embrasures are interproximal spaces where functional occlusal contact occurs, enabling controlled food movement. - These interactions stabilize chewing forces and prevent pathological stress distribution.  
Answer: C  

Educational significance: Emphasizing that guiding cusps engage in embrasures—not just stating it—deepens student comprehension by linking structural anatomy (interdental spaces) to dynamic biomechanics (functional occlusion). This enables accurate interpretation of diagnostic radiographs, precise restoration design (e.g., avoiding unsupported cuspal contacts), and proactive prevention of malocclusions or bruxism-related damage through evidence-based occlusal analysis.
